title: Fix Layout: mobile header clip, mobile menu, Stats in More menu, dropdown bleed-through, duplicate theme toggles, nav naming
description:
There are SIX issues to fix in this file:

### Issue 1: Mobile header logo text is clipped on the left edge
On mobile (375px width), the 'Psi Research Library' header logo text is clipped on the left side. Add appropriate padding-left or adjust the logo container's margin/padding at small breakpoints so the full text is visible.

### Issue 2: Mobile menu creates visual confusion with overlapping content
Opening the hamburger menu creates a dark side panel, but the underlying page content is still partially visible and overlapping, creating visual confusion. Add a proper overlay/backdrop behind the mobile menu panel to fully obscure the page content when the menu is open. This could be a semi-transparent dark overlay.

### Issue 3: 'More' dropdown is missing a 'Stats' link
The Stats page exists and is linked in the footer, but it's missing from the 'More' dropdown menu in the main navigation. Add { href: '/stats', label: '📊 Stats' } to the moreNavLinks array.

### Issue 4: Dropdown background bleed-through
When the 'More' dropdown is open, text from the page behind it (like 'Reading Path') is visible through/around the dropdown panel. Ensure the dropdown panel has a solid, opaque background (not transparent or semi-transparent) and proper z-index stacking.

### Issue 5: Two separate theme toggle buttons exist
There are both a 'Toggle dark mode' button and a separate 'Toggle Theme' button. Having two separate implementations is fragile and could desynchronize. Remove one of them — keep whichever is the primary one used in the header, and remove the duplicate.

### Issue 6: Nav says 'Methodology' but page is '/method' with title 'Library Analytics'
The 'More' dropdown link says 'Methodology' (with href '/method') but the page title is 'Library Analytics'. Rename the nav link label to 'Method' or 'Analytics' for consistency.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.

title: Fix timeline: 'River of Time' starts at year 0 and eras are out of order
description:
On the timeline page, the visualization header says '0 — 2026' rather than starting at 1882 or 1886 (the earliest era mentioned). The start year variable is probably defaulting to 0. Find where the start year is computed and fix it to use the actual earliest era start year from the data.

Also, the eras are displayed out of chronological order (Era 2 first, then Era 3, then Era 1). Sort the eras by their chronological start year before rendering them.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.
